question.text: Under SEBI (Depositories and Participants) Regulations 2018, what is the net worth requirement for a SEBI-registered stockbroker to become a depository participant within one year of notification?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A SEBI registered stockbroker can become a depository participant provided the stock broker shall have a net worth of Rs. 3 crore within one year of the date of notification of the amendment regulations, which shall be increased to Rs. 5 crore within two years. **The Trap:** Rs. 5 crore is the ultimate requirement after two years, not the initial requirement. The phased implementation allows time for stockbrokers to build up their net worth. **Cross-Exam Context:** This phased net worth requirement reflects SEBI's approach of gradual implementation. Compare with portfolio manager net worth (Rs. 5 crore) and merchant banker Category I (Rs. 50 crore). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A self-clearing member fulfilling the net worth requirements under SEBI (Stock Brokers) Regulations, 1992 is also eligible to register as a DP, providing an alternative entry path.
